children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The President shall designate in the Federal Emergency Management Agency a Small State and Rural Advocate.

children.heading: Responsibilities
children.label: b Responsibilities
children.text: The Small State and Rural Advocate shall be an advocate for the fair treatment of small States and rural communities in the provision of assistance under this chapter.

participate in the disaster declaration process under and the emergency declaration process under , to ensure that the needs of rural communities are being addressed;section 5170 of this titlesection 5191 of this title
assist small population States in the preparation of requests for major disaster or emergency declarations;

children.label: A
children.text: the per capita personal income by local area, as calculated by the Bureau of Economic Analysis;

children.label: i
children.text: the percentage of the population for whom poverty status is determined;

children.label: ii
children.text: the percentage of the population already receiving Government assistance such as Supplemental Security Income and Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program benefits;

children.label: iv
children.text: the percentage of the population that is 65 years old and older;

children.label: v
children.text: the percentage of the population 18 years old and younger;

children.label: vi
children.text: the percentage of the population with a disability;

children.label: vii
children.text: the percentage of the population who speak a language other than English and speak English less than “very well”; and

children.label: viii
children.text: any unique considerations regarding American Indian and Alaskan Native Tribal populations raised in the State’s request for a major disaster declaration that may not be reflected in the data points referenced in this subparagraph;

children.label: i
children.text: disruptions to community life-saving and life-sustaining services;

children.label: ii
children.text: disruptions or increased demand for essential community services; and

children.label: iii
children.text: disruptions to transportation, infrastructure, and utilities; and

children.label: D
children.text: any other information relevant to demonstrate severe local impacts; and
conduct such other activities as the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency considers appropriate.
